sheets-nate.text: Nate Sheets is a candidate for Texas Commissioner of Agriculture, running with Republican Party of Texas.
Nate Sheets's recorded background: Nate Sheets, beekeeper and small business owner.
As of 2026-02-21, Nate Sheets's campaign had raised $1,064,677 in campaign receipts and spent $1,238,317, per Texas Ethics Commission filings.

tucker-clayton.text: Clayton Tucker is a candidate for Texas Commissioner of Agriculture, running with Texas Democratic Party.

On healthcare, Clayton Tucker's recorded position: Clayton Tucker states that "it is the government’s responsibility to ensure everyone has health insurance" and pledges to use the Agriculture office to lower prices and expand rural hospitals.
Clayton Tucker's recorded background: Clayton Tucker, rancher and founder of the Texas Progressive Caucus.
As of 2026-02-21, Clayton Tucker's campaign had raised $17,611 in campaign receipts and spent $13,465, per Texas Ethics Commission filings.

base.summary: Texas Commissioner of Agriculture race: Nate Sheets, Clayton Tucker. This overview lists each candidate's party, background, and campaign finance where available in our data set.
base.caveats: Our data set for this race covers candidate name, party, incumbency, background summary, and campaign finance totals where a filing was found (federal candidates file with the FEC; Texas state offices file with the Texas Ethics Commission -- each finance bullet names which). We do not have recorded voting histories or issue-by-issue policy positions for these candidates in this data set.

Below is what we do have on each candidate's background and, where available, campaign finance.
renter_young_worker.caveats: We only show a connection to renter young worker concerns when a candidate's own recorded background in our data set names it directly (for example, a farming or small-business background). Where no such record exists, we say so rather than guess at a candidate's likely position.

tucker-clayton.text: On education, Clayton Tucker's recorded position: Tucker supports taxpayer-funded public education "through college," indicating strong backing for public-school funding rather than vouchers or charters.

As of 2026-02-21, Clayton Tucker's campaign had raised $17,611 in total, per Texas Ethics Commission filings. That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on property taxes or public school funding.
